perturbation.original_text: Either party may terminate this Agreement, without cause, upon ninety (90) days prior written notice to the other party, provided, however, that all outstanding Work Orders shall continue to be governed by the terms and conditions hereof.
perturbation.changed_text: Either party may terminate this Agreement, without cause, upon thirty (30) days prior written notice to the other party, provided, however, that all outstanding Work Orders shall continue to be governed by the terms and conditions hereof.
perturbation.explanation: Reducing the notice period for termination to 30 days could be problematic if it affects ongoing projects and the ability of either party to wind down operations appropriately. It may violate implied duties of good faith and fair dealing, especially if a longer period is considered reasonable for the type of services provided. Because VirtualScopics is located in New York, that is the state where the company must follow the law.
perturbation.law_explanation: NY UCC § 2-309 addresses the absence of specific time provisions, which may include termination clauses. Subsection (3) states that termination of a contract by one party requires 'reasonable notification' be received by the other party. Reducing the notice period to 30 days might be deemed unreasonable depending on the scope of work and industry standards, potentially violating this section.
perturbation.scraped_snippet_1: NYS Open Legislation | NYSenate.gov Sorry, you need to enable JavaScript to visit this website. Skip to main content Legislation Search OpenLegislation Statutes Search Term Search icon Search The Laws of New York Consolidated Laws of New York CHAPTER 38 Uniform Commercial Code ARTICLE 2 Sales PART 3 General Obligation and Construction of Contract previous SECTION 2-308 Absence of Specified Place for Delivery up PART 3 General Obligation and Construction of Contract next SECTION 2-310 Open Time for Payment or Running of Credit; Authority to Ship Under Reservation This entry was published on 2014-09-22 The selection dates indicate all change milestones for the entire volume, not just the location being viewed. Specifying a milestone date will retrieve the most recent version of the location before that date. See most recent version before or on: 2014-09-22 2014-12-26 2015-01-02 2015-02-20 2015-02-27 2016-05-20 2016-08-26 2016-09-16 2018-08-31 2018-10-05 2021-12-03 2022-01-28 2022-06-03 Share Facebook Email SECTION 2-309 Absence of Specific Time Provisions; Notice of Termination Uniform Commercial Code (UCC) CHAPTER 38, ARTICLE 2, PART 3 Section 2--309. Absence of Specific Time Provisions; Notice of Termination. (1) The time for shipment or delivery or any other action under a contract if not provided in this Article or agreed upon shall be a reasonable time. (2) Where the contract provides for successive performances but is indefinite in duration it is valid for a reasonable time but unless otherwise agreed may be terminated at any time by either party. (3) Termination of a contract by one party except on the happening of an agreed event requires that reasonable notification be received by the other party and an agreement dispensing with notification is invalid if its operation would be unconscionable.
perturbation.justification: The changed text reduces the termination notice period from 90 to 30 days, potentially contradicting NY UCC § 2-309, as explained in the Law Explanation. This section requires 'reasonable notification' for contract termination. Scraped Law Snippet 1, referencing NY UCC § 2-309, states that 'Termination of a contract by one party... requires that reasonable notification be received by the other party'. The Perturbation Explanation also suggests this reduction may violate implied duties of good faith. The change introduces an inconsistency because the 30-day notice might not be deemed 'reasonable' depending on the project scope, making it an Inconsistencies Legal Contradiction.
